task: I need help drafting a short message to a vendor about a paperwork mismatch on an order. The message should stay focused on the order details and leave room for me to fill in the specifics afterward.

prefs.explanation: He seems most comfortable with a businesslike interaction style rather than friendliness for its own sake. Given his contract-focused, guarded approach, overly casual wording would likely feel sloppy or untrustworthy. He would probably respond best to a composed tone that stays respectful and efficient.

prefs.short: Wants concise answers with no padding
prefs.explanation: He likely prefers responses that get to the point quickly and avoid unnecessary commentary. His practical, control-oriented style suggests low patience for rambling explanations or emotional framing. If a topic needs detail, he would still want it trimmed to only what changes the decision.

prefs.short: Likes bullet points and structured output
prefs.explanation: He appears to favor information that is organized, easy to scan, and hard to misread. Lists, checklists, or clearly separated sections would fit his record-keeping habits better than loose prose. Unstructured answers probably feel inefficient to him, especially when he is trying to act on something.

He seems unlikely to want hedging or excessive politeness when the point is clarity. His guarded pragmatism fits a style that states the issue plainly, especially when there is risk or cost involved. Softened language may read to him as evasive or manipulative rather than considerate.

prefs.short: Wants firm pushback on bad calls
prefs.explanation: He is probably receptive to being challenged when the challenge is concrete and tied to risk. Because he already thinks in terms of exposure and downside, he may value an assistant that flags weak assumptions instead of simply agreeing. He would likely dislike vague disagreement, but accept hard pushback if it is specific and justified.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a short fill-in template:

> Hi, I’m reviewing order **[order number]** and found a mismatch between the paperwork and the order details. Please confirm the correct **[item / quantity / price / date / other detail]** in writing so I can update my records. I’m holding off on any next step until the order information is matched up.

If you want, I can make it **firmer** or **more neutral**.

per_pref.reason: Uses a neutral, professional tone: "Hi, I’m reviewing order..." and "Please confirm... in writing". It is composed and businesslike, though the opening "Sure" is slightly casual.
Very concise and to the point. It gives a short template with no padding and only the needed action items.
Provides a structured fill-in template with placeholders like "[order number]" and "[item / quantity / price / date / other detail]". It is organized and easy to scan, though not in bullet points.
Fairly direct and unsoftened: "found a mismatch" and "Please confirm... in writing". It avoids hedging, though it is not especially blunt.

Includes mild pushback by stating "I’m holding off on any next step until the order information is matched up," which flags a consequence and sets a boundary. It does not strongly challenge the vendor, but it does not պարզապես agree either.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here are two short options.

**Plain:**
> Order [#] has a paperwork mismatch. Please confirm the correct [detail] in writing so I can update the record. I’m not moving forward until the paperwork matches the order.

**Firmer:**
> Order [#] does not match the paperwork. Confirm the correct [detail] in writing. I will not move forward until the order record is corrected.

If you want, I can make it even shorter or swap in a more formal tone.

per_pref.reason: Uses a neutral, professional tone: "Please confirm" and "I will not move forward" are composed and businesslike. Slightly conversational opener "Sure — here are two short options" keeps it from being fully formal.
Very concise and to the point. It offers only two short templates with no padding or extra explanation beyond a brief offer to adjust tone/length.
Strongly structured with clear bullets and labeled options: "Plain:" and "Firmer:". Easy to scan and directly usable.
Provides blunt, unsoftened wording such as "does not match the paperwork" and "I will not move forward until the order record is corrected." No hedging or excessive politeness.

Includes firm pushback by stating "I’m not moving forward until the paperwork matches the order" and "I will not move forward until the order record is corrected." It does not explicitly challenge assumptions beyond the mismatch itself, so not a perfect 5.
